title: Nouns in context (A2)
lesson: Choose the noun that best fits each everyday situation. These words appear often in travel, work, and daily life.

items.text: The {1} is terrible today, with heavy rain and strong wind.
items.explanation: Weather describes conditions like rain and wind.

items.text: Our {1} from London to Paris took two hours by train.
items.explanation: A journey is the act of travelling from one place to another.

items.text: You need a {1} to get on the train.
items.explanation: A ticket gives you permission to travel.

items.text: My teacher gave me good {1} about studying.
items.explanation: Advice is an opinion about what someone should do.

items.text: The family next door is my new {1}.
items.explanation: A neighbour lives near you.

items.text: The shop {1} decides who works each day.
items.explanation: A manager is the person in charge of a workplace.

items.text: I followed the {1} carefully to bake the cake.
items.explanation: A recipe gives instructions for cooking food.

items.text: There was a lot of {1} on the road, so we arrived late.
items.explanation: Traffic is the cars and vehicles on the road.

items.text: We carried our heavy {1} to the hotel room.
items.explanation: Luggage is the bags you take when travelling.

items.text: I made an {1} to see the dentist on Friday.
items.explanation: An appointment is an arranged time to meet someone.

items.text: My favourite {1} is collecting old coins.
items.explanation: A hobby is an activity you do for fun in your free time.

items.text: The waiter brought the {1} so we could pay.
items.explanation: A bill shows how much money you must pay.

items.text: The plane landed safely at the {1}.
items.explanation: An airport is the place where planes take off and land.

items.text: I sent a {1} to my friend abroad with a stamp on it.
items.explanation: A letter is a written message you send by post.

items.text: The doctor wrote a {1} for my medicine.
items.explanation: A prescription tells the chemist which medicine you need.

items.text: We booked a {1} at a hotel near the beach.
items.explanation: A reservation is an arrangement to keep something for you.

items.text: I keep all my appointments written in my {1}.
items.explanation: A diary is a book where you note your plans and days.

items.text: The teacher gave the class homework as an {1}.
items.explanation: An exercise is a task you do to practise and learn.

items.text: The shop is having a {1}, so everything is cheaper.
items.explanation: A sale is a time when goods are sold at lower prices.

items.text: I paid for the shopping with my credit {1}.
items.explanation: A card is a small plastic item used to pay for things.

items.text: The {1} on the train checked all our tickets.
items.explanation: A conductor checks tickets on a train.

items.text: She works as a {1} in a busy hospital.
items.explanation: A nurse looks after sick people in a hospital.

items.text: Every {1} on the bus had to wear a seatbelt.
items.explanation: A passenger is a person travelling in a vehicle.

items.text: The {1} complained that the food was cold.
items.explanation: A customer is a person who buys something in a shop or restaurant.

items.text: I read the morning {1} to learn the news.
items.explanation: A newspaper contains the latest news.

items.text: We watched the football {1} at the stadium.
items.explanation: A match is a sports game between two teams.

items.text: The {1} of the building is on the ground floor.
items.explanation: An entrance is the place where you go into a building.

items.text: I lost my {1}, so I cannot get into my flat.
items.explanation: A key opens a lock to let you in.

items.text: There is a long {1} of people at the bank.
items.explanation: A queue is a line of people waiting their turn.

items.text: The {1} between the two cities is about 50 kilometres.
items.explanation: Distance is how far apart two places are.

items.text: She gave a clear {1} on how to find the museum.
items.explanation: A direction tells you which way to go.

items.text: I need to fill in this {1} to open a bank account.
items.explanation: A form is a document with spaces for your information.

items.text: The waiter recommended the fish as today's {1}.
items.explanation: A dish is a particular type of prepared food.

items.text: We took lots of {1} of the mountains on our holiday.
items.explanation: Photographs are pictures taken with a camera.
